findings.title: Side-effectful route registry must include new mutation endpoints
findings.body: A dashboard mutation endpoint that is not registered here would miss the Origin gate, so every new POST or DELETE route needs a registry update in the same patch.
findings.trustBoundary: browser origin to local privileged server
findings.attackerPreconditions: User has an active dashboard server and visits a malicious same-network or browser page that can target localhost.
findings.exploitability: Requires a future route omission; current registry is explicit and audited by review.
findings.blastRadius: Local project state or terminal sessions could be changed without the intended same-origin check.
findings.proofOfFix: Add a regression that asserts every mutating API route is included in the side-effectful route registry.

attackPathSummary: A malicious page targets an unregistered future mutation route and bypasses the Origin check.
falsePositivesRemoved: Static asset reads are excluded because they do not mutate local state.
positiveObservations: Existing terminal creation is already listed in the side-effectful route registry.

activeTestingGate.reason: No Critical or High finding is present in this fixture.
